title: ErrorJson
description: Serializable error envelope emitted as a single NDJSON line.

best_candidate.description: Closest near-miss when a match cascade fails (v0.1.29 P0-2).

error.description: Always true, marks this line as an error event.

error_class.description: Error class: transient, conflict, `precondition_failed`, or permanent.

failed_pair_index.description: 1-based index of the failed `--old`/`--new` pair (multi-pair edit, G117).

pair_results.description:
Per-pair diagnostics up to and including the failed pair (multi-pair edit, G117).
Pairs after the failure were never attempted and are absent.

pairs_total.description: Total number of `--old`/`--new` pairs in the invocation (multi-pair edit, G117).

path.description: Filesystem path related to the error, if applicable.

retryable.description: Whether a retry may resolve this error.

suggestion.description: Optional actionable suggestion for the caller.

workspace.description: Workspace root used for jail validation, if applicable.

BestCandidate.description:
Closest near-miss when a fuzzy/exact match fails (v0.1.29 P0-2).

Agents use this to correct `old` without re-reading the full file.

line.description: 1-based line of the candidate start in the file.

similarity.description: Similarity score 0.0–1.0 of the best attempt.

strategy.description: Strategy that produced this candidate score.

text.description: Snippet of the best matching region (truncated to 500 chars).

PairResult.description:
Outcome of matching a single `--old`/`--new` pair in multi-pair edit mode.

Emitted in `pair_results` on both success and error envelopes so agents
can verify each pair objectively without re-running the edit (G117).
In the default all-or-nothing mode, pairs after the first failure are
never attempted and are absent from the array.

index.description: 1-based position of the pair in the `--old`/`--new` invocation order.

matched.description: Whether the pair matched and was applied to the content.

similarity.description: Similarity score of the fuzzy match, 0.0-1.0 (block-anchor and context-aware only).

source.description: Source of the match/replacement content: `"arg"` or `"file"`.
